items.title: [正面] *This IS a gacha game, and while it's a very ftp f...
items.summary:
*This IS a gacha game, and while it's a very ftp friendly one, it's still important to point out as much. 

The story of Morimens both in terms of development and the in-game story itself has fascinated me. The setting itself fully embraces dark, eldritch tones, disturbing depictions, and various other forms of potentially unsettling content, yet... the key guides the way. 

To clarify, I'm only up to chapter 5 so far (my playtime is also a lie, as I've mostly played on mobile up until now). There's a clear thread of hope, making the best of a tragic situation despite the odds, that ties the story together.

The gameplay as well supports the oppressive yet hopeful atmosphere of the game. It's very much a Slay The Spire like, to the point I originally wrote the game off because of it; I'm still regretting that decision. Giving Morimens a fair shot reminded me of my first time playing Slay The Spire. Everything was a discovery, I was constantly itching to try the next dungeon, and teambuilding (even with a smol roster) was endlessly exciting.
There's simply a special feeling that comes from turning a seemingly hopeless turn into clutch victory.

It's a shame it still hasn't gotten the marketing it deserves, but if any of this interest you... please, give this game a try. For a genre so full of soulless cash-grabs and genshin-clones, it's refreshing to have one that's just a good game.
